title: rubus lithuanicus
intro: Rubus lithuanicus, also known as the Lithuanian bramble or black raspberry, is a type of thorny shrub native to Eastern Europe, particularly in Lithuania, Poland, and Belarus. The plant is rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that exhibit medicinal properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. Consuming Rubus lithuanicus has been linked to several health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rubus lithuanicus are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make jams, jellies, and other culinary products, as well as for medicinal purposes.
